materials.name: Small Log
materials.description: A short section of tree trunk left behind after felling a tree, the base feedstock that gets processed into usable Lumber.

sources.detail: Use Cut on trees to fell them; Small Logs are dropped in wooded areas across multiple regions.

materials.usedInSummary: Feedstock for Lumber; also usable as-is in some early recipes.

materials.name: Sturdy Stick
materials.description: A fallen branch sturdy enough to use directly in simple toy and tool recipes.

sources.detail: Picked up near trees while exploring, or obtained with Cut on wooden debris.

materials.usedInSummary: Early crafting material for toys and simple tools; also the gift Scyther requests before teaching Cut.

materials.name: Lumber
materials.description: Milled, construction-ready wood processed from raw logs rather than gathered directly.

sources.detail: Give Small Logs to a Pokémon with the Chop specialty (e.g. Scyther, Pinsir, Heracross) to process them into Lumber.

materials.usedInSummary: Core building block for early furniture and wooden structures.

materials.name: Vine Rope
materials.description: A length of tough plant vine, twisted enough to double as cordage.

sources.detail: Use Cut on vines, which grow in caves and high-altitude areas.
Dropped by Bellsprout and Weepinbell in their habitats.

materials.usedInSummary: Binding material for rustic furniture and early recipes.

materials.name: Stone
materials.description: A common fist-sized rock, the most basic building material in the game.

sources.detail: Pick up loose stones while exploring, or use Rock Smash on boulders and rock blocks.

materials.usedInSummary: Ubiquitous base material for early building recipes; also the gift Hitmonchan requests before teaching Rock Smash.

materials.name: Limestone
materials.description: A pale sedimentary rock mined in bulk for Concrete and marble-style flooring.

sources.detail: Rock Smash limestone blocks found in the western cave of Rocky Ridges.
Also found on the eastern island of Sparkling Skylands.

materials.usedInSummary: Bulk mid-game resource for Concrete and marble-look building kits.

materials.name: Crystal Fragment
materials.description: A shard chipped from a larger mineral cluster, prized for gem-themed decor.

sources.detail: Rock Smash large crystal clusters found in the Rocky Ridges mines.

materials.usedInSummary: Decorative, rarer-tier material for gem and crystal-themed crafts.

materials.name: Soft Clay
materials.description: Wet, pliable clay dug from riverbanks and cave floors, the raw input for Brick.

sources.detail: Rock Smash clay blocks found on Bleak Beach and in nearby caves such as Onix's cavern.
Occasionally left behind by Ground-type Pokémon with the Litter specialty.

materials.usedInSummary: Feedstock for Brick; also used directly in a few clay-themed recipes.

materials.name: Brick
materials.description: Fired clay block used for sturdy walls, roads, and roofing.

sources.detail: Give Soft Clay to a Pokémon with the Burn specialty to bake it into Brick.

materials.usedInSummary: Core building material for stone-look houses and paths.

materials.name: Copper Ore
materials.description: Reddish raw ore that yields Copper Ingot once smelted.

sources.detail: Rock Smash copper ore blocks found in the Rocky Ridges mines.

materials.usedInSummary: Early-game metal source for Copper Ingot.

materials.name: Iron Ore
materials.description: Dense grey ore that smelts into Iron Ingot, a staple of mid-game construction.

sources.detail: Rock Smash iron ore blocks found in the Rocky Ridges mines.
Give Nonburnable Garbage to a Pokémon with the Recycle specialty in exchange for Iron Ore.

materials.usedInSummary: Feedstock for Iron Ingot, used across furniture, tools, and machine parts.

materials.name: Gold Ore
materials.description: A rare golden ore, scarcer than Copper or Iron Ore.

sources.detail: Rock Smash gold ore blocks in the Rocky Ridges mines; rarer than Copper or Iron Ore.

materials.usedInSummary: Feedstock for Gold Ingot, used in luxury decor.

materials.name: Pokemetal Fragment
materials.description: A chunk of exotic ore unique to Pokopia's ruined biomes.

sources.detail: Rock Smash Pokemetal ore blocks found in Withered Wasteland mines.
Also found in Sparkling Skylands mines.

materials.usedInSummary: Feedstock for Pokemetal, used in advanced crafting.

materials.name: Copper Ingot
materials.description: A smelted copper bar used in early metal furniture and machine parts.

sources.detail: Smelt Copper Ore in a Smelting Furnace with a Burn-specialty Pokémon.

materials.usedInSummary: Metal component for early machines and decor.

materials.name: Iron Ingot
materials.description: A smelted iron bar, a workhorse metal for tools, machines, and the Concrete Mixer.

sources.detail: Smelt Iron Ore in a Smelting Furnace with a Burn-specialty Pokémon.

materials.usedInSummary: Key structural metal; needed to build the Concrete Mixer and iron-themed furniture.

materials.name: Gold Ingot
materials.description: A smelted gold bar prized for shiny, high-tier decor.

sources.detail: Smelt Gold Ore in a Smelting Furnace with a Burn-specialty Pokémon.

materials.usedInSummary: Luxury metal for late-game decorative recipes.

materials.name: Pokemetal
materials.description: A smelted exotic alloy used in some of the game's most advanced recipes.

sources.detail: Smelt Pokemetal Fragments in a Smelting Furnace with a Burn-specialty Pokémon.

materials.usedInSummary: High-tier metal for advanced machines and armor-style crafts.

materials.name: Twine
materials.description: Coarse cordage salvaged from scavenged fabric, useful for rustic and woven-style crafts.

sources.detail: Use Cut on textile garbage and cobwebs found around abandoned buildings.
Dropped passively by Bug-type Pokémon with the Litter specialty, such as Spinarak and Ariados.
Purchasable in small bundles from Pokémon Center shops.
sources.notes: Price and shop availability not independently confirmed.

materials.usedInSummary: Woven-craft material for furniture and toys.

materials.name: Fluff
materials.description: Soft wool-like tufts shed passively by Wool Pokémon.

sources.detail: Dropped passively by Mareep and Flaaffy with the Litter specialty.

materials.usedInSummary: Soft-furnishing material for cushions and plush decor.

materials.name: Cloth
materials.description: A scrap of woven fabric salvaged from ruined buildings.

sources.detail: Use Cut on fabric piles and cobwebs in and around abandoned buildings.
sources.notes: Also reported as findable while exploring more generally; region confidence is moderate.

materials.usedInSummary: Fabric material for cloth walls and soft furnishings.

materials.name: Leaf
materials.description: A bright green leaf stripped from grass or trees.

sources.detail: Use Cut on tall grass and trees, or pick leaves up directly while exploring.
Dropped by Venusaur with the Litter specialty.

materials.usedInSummary: Common decorative and plant-craft material.

materials.name: Wastepaper
materials.description: Grimy discarded paper, worthless until recycled.

sources.detail: Scattered on the ground throughout Sparkling Skylands.

materials.usedInSummary: Sole feedstock for clean Paper.

materials.name: Paper
materials.description: Clean white paper recycled from scavenged Wastepaper.

sources.detail: Give Wastepaper to a Pokémon with the Recycle specialty (e.g. Trubbish, Garbodor, Porygon).

materials.usedInSummary: Used in craft and decor recipes that call for paper goods.

materials.name: Concrete
materials.description: A hardened building compound mixed from crushed Limestone.

sources.detail: Build a Concrete Mixer (2 Iron Ingots), load it with Limestone near water, and have a Crush-specialty Pokémon process it.
sources.notes: The Concrete Mixer recipe is unlocked through Sparkling Skylands progression.

materials.usedInSummary: Modern building material for sturdy floors and walls.

materials.name: Glass
materials.description: Clear melted glass used for windows and glassware.

sources.detail: Smelt Volcanic Ash or Sand in a Smelting Furnace with a Burn-specialty Pokémon.
Occasionally obtained by smashing broken windows.

materials.usedInSummary: Used for windows and glass-themed decor.

materials.name: Paint
materials.description: Colored dye crushed from berries, used to recolor buildings and furniture.

sources.detail: Give a berry to a Pokémon with the Crush specialty (e.g. Onix, Tyranitar) and request Paint; the berry's color determines the paint color (Leppa -> Red, Chesto -> Blue, Lum -> Green, Aspear -> Yellow, Pecha -> Pink, Rawst -> Cyan).
sources.notes: White and Black Paint are rare bonus drops from certain berries rather than tied to one dedicated berry.

materials.usedInSummary: Recolors buildings, blocks, and furniture; each color is a distinct, non-interchangeable item.

materials.name: Seashell
materials.description: A common shell washed up on the shoreline.

sources.detail: Pick up along the Bleak Beach shoreline.

materials.usedInSummary: Beach-themed decor and building material.

materials.name: Sea Glass
materials.description: A smooth, frosted shard of glass tumbled by the surf.

sources.detail: Pick up while exploring Bleak Beach, often on tide-washed tiles.

materials.usedInSummary: Decorative beach-craft material.

materials.name: Nonburnable Garbage
materials.description: Rusted, unburnable trash cleared from the coastline.

sources.detail: Pick up while exploring Bleak Beach.

materials.usedInSummary: Trade-in item exchanged for Iron Ore via a Recycle-specialty Pokémon.

materials.name: Glowing Mushroom
materials.description: A bioluminescent fungus found underground, inedible but handy as a light source.

sources.detail: Found inside caves, notably in Withered Wasteland.
Also found inside Rocky Ridges caves.

materials.usedInSummary: Light-themed decor material.

materials.name: Honey
materials.description: Sweet, sticky honey that also lures certain Pokémon back to their homes.

sources.detail: Inspect sparkling flower beds while exploring.
Given by Combee's Gather specialty.

materials.usedInSummary: Food/lure item and ingredient in a handful of recipes.

materials.name: Volcanic Ash
materials.description: Fine grey ash collected near volcanic vents, a key ingredient for Glass.

sources.detail: Found in volcanic areas.
sources.notes: Specific region uncertain across sources; Sand can substitute for it in the same recipe.

materials.usedInSummary: Primary feedstock for Glass alongside Sand.

materials.name: Leppa Berry
materials.description: A red berry that grows naturally in every save file, useful both as a cooking ingredient and as the Red dye source for Paint.

sources.detail: Headbutt the naturally-growing Leppa Berry tree; unlike the other five berries, it needs no seed and is guaranteed to grow in every save.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Leppa Salad and Leppa Bread; also the Red dye source when given to a Crush-specialty Pokémon for Paint.

materials.name: Chesto Berry
materials.description: A blue berry cultivated from seeds found near Smeargle's hut on Bleak Beach.

sources.detail: Find Chesto Berry seeds in a Poké Ball near Smeargle's hut, plant and grow the tree, then headbutt it once ripe.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Crushed-Berry Salad; also the Blue dye source when given to a Crush-specialty Pokémon for Paint.

materials.name: Aspear Berry
materials.description: A yellow berry cultivated from seeds found near Bleak Beach's waterfall.

sources.detail: Find Aspear Berry seeds in a Poké Ball near the Bleak Beach waterfall, plant and grow the tree, then headbutt it once ripe.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Flavorful Soup; also the Yellow dye source when given to a Crush-specialty Pokémon for Paint.

materials.name: Pecha Berry
materials.description: A pink berry cultivated from seeds found along the Lava Lake side path in Rocky Ridges.

sources.detail: Find Pecha Berry seeds in a Poké Ball on the Lava Lake side path (reachable with an upgraded Cut), plant and grow the tree, then headbutt it once ripe.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Fluffy Bread; also the Pink dye source when given to a Crush-specialty Pokémon for Paint.

materials.name: Rawst Berry
materials.description: A cyan berry cultivated from seeds hidden among hanging vines on Sparkling Skylands' upper-left island.

sources.detail: Find Rawst Berry seeds in a Poké Ball tucked in hanging vines on Sparkling Skylands' upper-left island, plant and grow the tree, then headbutt it once ripe.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Bitter Hamburger Steak; also the Cyan dye source when given to a Crush-specialty Pokémon for Paint.

materials.name: Lum Berry
materials.description: A green berry cultivated from seeds found past Gimmighoul's Manor in Rocky Ridges.

sources.detail: Find Lum Berry seeds in a Poké Ball past Gimmighoul's Manor, in the lava area of Rocky Ridges, plant and grow the tree, then headbutt it once ripe.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Bitter Hamburger Steak; also the Green dye source when given to a Crush-specialty Pokémon for Paint.

materials.name: Wheat
materials.description: A staple grain crop tilled and grown to bake into bread.

sources.detail: Till soil with Rototiller and plant Wheat Seeds, which turn up as Pokémon gifts, in red Poké Balls, or from water splashes around Rocky Ridges; harvest once ripe.
sources.notes: Sparkling Skylands' enriched soil also grows Wheat, roughly 15% faster than other regions; whether watering is required was not confirmed across sources.

materials.usedInSummary: Base cooking ingredient for every bread recipe, cooked at the kitchen to restore Cut PP.

materials.name: Bean
materials.description: A hardy legume crop that grows without watering, the base cooking ingredient for hamburger steaks.

sources.detail: Till soil with Rototiller and plant Bean Seeds bought from the Withered Wasteland Pokémon Center shop; Beans grow on their own without needing to be watered.
sources.notes: regions.json currently lists 'beans' (plural) under Rocky Ridges' keyResources instead of Withered Wasteland; this file uses the singular id 'bean' per instruction, matching the ingredient id already used throughout recipes.json. Region flagged for reconciliation.

materials.usedInSummary: Base cooking ingredient for every hamburger steak recipe, cooked at the kitchen to restore Rock Smash PP.

materials.name: Fresh Carrot
materials.description: A carrot pulled up by its leaves from soft ground, found wild or grown from seed.

sources.detail: Pull ripe Fresh Carrots directly from soft ground; no tool is required.
Also spawns naturally and can be pulled from the ground in Palette Town.
sources.notes: One quest walkthrough source also describes obtaining a Fresh Carrot by 'randomly digging around' or as a gift from another player, without naming a specific region.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Carrot Bread; also a gift that unlocks Gulpin's storage feature.

materials.name: Tomato
materials.description: A red garden vegetable that needs regular watering to ripen.

sources.detail: Till soil with Rototiller and plant Tomato Seeds, bought from the Pokémon Center shop after raising Bleak Beach's environment level, then water regularly with Water Gun until ripe.
sources.notes: regions.json currently lists tomato under Sparkling Skylands' keyResources rather than Bleak Beach; flagged for reconciliation. Tomato is also the gift Paldean Wooper requests (3 Tomatoes) to teach its liquid-carrying move.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Tomato Hamburger Steak.

materials.name: Potato
materials.description: A starchy root vegetable grown from seed in tilled soil.

sources.detail: Use Cut on wild potato plants around Sparkling Skylands to collect Potato Seeds, then till soil with Rototiller, plant, and water with Water Gun until ripe.
sources.notes: Sourcing is mixed: Serebii's item database instead lists Potato as a natural spawn on a 'Dream Islands' sub-area not otherwise documented among Pokopia's core regions, while Game8's seed guide points to Sparkling Skylands. This entry follows the farmable-crop path in Sparkling Skylands as the more consistent, reachable source.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Potato Hamburger Steak and Vibrant Hamburger Steak.

materials.name: Cave Mushroom
materials.description: A pale fungus that grows in damp, shaded ground and doubles as a mild PP-restoring snack.

sources.detail: Found growing in small numbers on damp, shaded ground on Rocky Ridges, including its green mountaintop areas.
sources.notes: regions.json currently cross-references this material to rocky-ridges-underground, but both Game8's guide and Serebii's item database place it on the Rocky Ridges surface rather than specifically underground; flagged for reconciliation. Separately, recipes.json's mushroom-soup and mushroom-hamburger-steak entries reference the plural id 'cave-mushrooms', which does not match this file's singular id 'cave-mushroom' used per instruction — flagging this id mismatch for reconciliation.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Mushroom Soup and Mushroom Hamburger Steak; also restores some PP if eaten raw.

materials.name: Seaweed
materials.description: Strands of dried seaweed washed up along the shoreline.

sources.detail: Pick up directly off the ground along the southern beach area of Bleak Beach; no tool is required.

materials.usedInSummary: Cooking ingredient in Seaweed Salad and Seaweed Soup.

materials.name: Fresh Water
materials.description: A bottled portion of clean water, either bought or randomly dispensed from a working vending machine.

sources.detail: Purchase from the PC Shop for 50 Life Coins after reaching Environment Level 3 in Rocky Ridges.
Random daily drop from powered vending machines in Withered Wasteland; the machine must have electricity to dispense anything.

materials.usedInSummary: Base liquid ingredient for every soup recipe; also what the Suck move carries and places as water blocks.
